title: tryonella lonchophora
intro: Tryonella lonchophora, also known as Lomatium, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family, native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The medicinal properties of Tryonella lonchophora have been used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, and it is also believed to have antiviral and antifungal properties. Tryonella lonchophora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support immune function and overall health.
